name: read
notes: VFS-backed; ext4 read path goes through `kernel::pagecache` (4 KiB inode-id-keyed LRU). Pipe / socket / fifo reads are signal-interruptible â€” return `-EINTR` on a deliverable signal during park.

name: write
notes: VFS-backed; ext4 `write_at` invalidates overlapping page-cache entries. Pipe / socket writes signal-interruptible. A `BrokenPipe` write sends `SIGPIPE` to the caller before returning `-EPIPE` â€” the documented dual contract; `cmd | head -N` patterns don't hang. The SIGPIPE half lives in `write_err_to_errno` and threads through `pwrite64` / `writev` / `pwritev` / `pwritev2` / `sendfile` / `splice` / `vmsplice` / `tee` / `copy_file_range`.

name: open
notes: Wraps `openat(AT_FDCWD, â€¦)`. The legacy nameless `open(2)`; modern userland uses `openat`.

name: close
notes: Drops the FD slot; OpenFile RC frees the inode handle.

name: stat
notes: Routes through `resolve_path` + `pack_stat`. ABI 144-byte `struct stat` with full nlink/uid/gid/timestamps/blocks.

name: fstat
notes: Fills `struct stat` (mode/size/kind only â€” uid/gid/atime are zeros).

name: lstat
notes: Same as `stat` but doesn't follow the final symlink (path resolver returns the Symlink inode itself).

name: poll
notes: Per-fd `revents` from `Inode::poll().bits()`. Timeout via `kernel::timeout` deadline-park. Multi-fd-wait scans on each tick (10ms granularity, matching the documented `poll(2)` `timeout` argument's millisecond resolution and the Hz=100 wakeup floor). Cap of 64 fds matches typical container-runtime usage.

name: lseek
notes: `SEEK_SET/CUR/END`.

name: mmap
notes: VMA-tracked, lazy fault-in via `kernel::mmap_fault` hook. `MAP_FIXED` / `MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE` / addr-hint / arena-fallback all honored. Anonymous + file-backed MAP_PRIVATE + MAP_SHARED. MAP_SHARED file maps pin page-cache frames directly; writes through mappings land in the cache and `msync` writes back. `mremap` is the separate `mremap` row. `MAP_GROWSDOWN` accepted-not-honored. munmap unpins SHARED-file pages.

name: mprotect
notes: `VmSpace::change_perms` walks 4 KiB pages and rewrites leaf-PTE flags; TLB shootdown after. PROT_NONE leaves PRESENT set (we don't yet clear it â€” the absence of READ alone doesn't unmap). PROT_GROWSUP / PROT_GROWSDOWN return EINVAL.

name: munmap
notes: Frees physical frames + invalidates local TLB; cross-CPU shootdown wired. Iterates page-by-page over the requested range, skipping unmapped pages â€” partial unmaps and over-aligned ranges both work.

name: brk
notes: Per-process heap window, clamped to `Process::brk.max`.

name: rt_sigaction
notes: Reads/writes the full 32-byte `struct kernel_sigaction { handler, flags, restorer, mask }`. `Process.sigactions[i]` stores all four fields. SA_NODEFER, SA_RESETHAND, SA_RESTORER, **SA_SIGINFO**, **SA_RESTART**, **SA_ONSTACK** all honored at delivery. SIGKILL/SIGSTOP can't be set. `sigsetsize` must be 8.

name: rt_sigprocmask
notes: SIG_BLOCK / SIG_UNBLOCK / SIG_SETMASK against `Process.blocked_signals`. SIGKILL and SIGSTOP can't be blocked even if userland tries. `sigsetsize` must be 8.

name: rt_sigreturn
notes: Reads the ABI `ucontext_t` (936 B; sigcontext at offset 40, sigmask at 296, fpregs reserved at 424) from user `rsp`, restores every saved register into `tf`, restores the pre-handler blocked-signal mask. The signal frame is bit-for-bit the ABI `rt_sigframe` shape. Dispatcher skips post-syscall `deliver_pending_signals` after sigreturn so the restored `rax` survives back to userland verbatim.

name: ioctl
notes: TTY ioctls (`TIOCGWINSZ`, `TCGETS`/`TCSETS{,W,F}` with proper 36-byte termios, `TIOCGPGRP`/`TIOCSPGRP`/`TIOCSCTTY`), virtio-input ioctls. `ENOTTY` for unknown ops on TTY/CharDevice fds + non-CharDevice fds â€” the documented contract for an unsupported request on an otherwise-valid fd. New ioctl families wire as workloads exercise them.

name: access
notes: Real per-mode check via `Credentials::can_access` against the inode's owner/group/mode. ABI semantics: uses *real* (`ruid`/`rgid`) creds, not effective. Privileged caller bypasses (CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE-style).

name: pipe
notes: Reuses `pipe2` with flags=0.

name: select
notes: fd_set bitmaps marshalled to/from a pollfd buffer and waited via the shared `poll_wait` core (the lost-wake-guarded loop factored out of `poll`). Returns the count of ready (fd,set) bits; POLLNVAL in a set â†’ EBADF. timeout is a `timeval` (NULL = block forever). Cap 64 fds.

name: sched_yield
notes: Schedules into the per-CPU runqueue.

name: mremap
notes: Anon-VMA shrink + extend-in-place + `MREMAP_MAYMOVE` relocate. Whole-VMA only (partial-range remap = EINVAL); file-backed grow = EINVAL (deferred). `MREMAP_FIXED` / `MREMAP_DONTUNMAP` = EINVAL. Copy via `frame::user::copy_{from,to}_user` through a 4 KiB chunk so `kernel/` keeps `forbid(unsafe_code)`. Cgroup uncharged on shrink + relocate.

name: msync
notes: Walks SHARED file VMAs in `[addr, addr+length)` and calls `pagecache::writeback` for each. Conservative dirty-tracking (every writable SHARED faulted page is dirty until written back). MS_SYNC / MS_ASYNC / MS_INVALIDATE all map to synchronous writeback.

name: mincore
notes: Walks per-process VMAs, validates range coverage (-ENOMEM on holes), then for each page calls `VmSpace::translate` and writes 1 if mapped, 0 if not. Page-aligned addr required. Per `mincore(2)`: byte 0 of each output records the "resident" bit; other bits zero.

name: madvise
notes: Accept-and-ignore for all hints â€” the common hints are advisory no-ops per `madvise(2)`. `MADV_DONTNEED` / `MADV_FREE` would benefit from lazy per-page reclaim we don't have yet. Wire DONTNEED to drop matching frames when an allocator-shaped workload demands it.

name: shmget
notes: Real SysV shm via `kernel::ipc::shm`. Allocates `Vec<PhysFrame>` per segment, zero-init, IPC_PRIVATE / IPC_CREAT / IPC_EXCL / key-based lookup, permission check from `creds`. SHMMAX = 1 GiB.

name: shmat
notes: Maps the segment's frames into the calling process via `vm.map_one_frame` (no fresh alloc â€” the frames are owned by the segment). Records a `VmaBacking::Shm` so `shmdt` / `munmap` can identify it. Multi-process sharing works because the same physical frames are mapped into multiple page tables. SHM_RDONLY honored.

name: shmctl
notes: IPC_RMID marks for deletion (segment dies when `attached==0`); IPC_STAT / IPC_SET accept-noop (typical SysV-shm users don't read the shmid_ds).

name: pause
notes: Pre-park signal-pending check + park on `signalfd_waiters`; any signal post wakes the parker. Always returns -EINTR per `pause(2)`.

name: nanosleep
notes: Real timer-driven park â€” registers a deadline on `kernel::timeout`, parks via `sched::park_self`; the LAPIC tick handler walks the expired-deadlines list and wakes us. Signal-interruptible: returns `-EINTR` and writes the remaining time to `rem` if the caller passed one.

name: getitimer
notes: Real ITIMER_REAL query: returns `(interval_ns, remaining_ns)` from `Process.itimer_real_interval_ns` / `_deadline_ns`, encoded as `struct itimerval`. ITIMER_VIRTUAL / PROF return zeros (no per-task user/system CPU split yet).

name: alarm
notes: Wraps the existing ITIMER_REAL machinery: records the new deadline on `Process.itimer_real_deadline_ns`, registers `itimer_real_fire` callback via `crate::timeout`, returns the previously-armed alarm's seconds-remaining. `alarm(0)` cancels.

name: setitimer
notes: Real ITIMER_REAL arming: `value_ns=0` disarms; non-zero registers a `crate::timeout` callback at `now + value_ns` keyed by pid that posts SIGALRM and (if `interval_ns != 0`) re-registers itself for the next period. CBS-style drift-free advance. Cancelled on execve, exit, or fresh setitimer. ITIMER_VIRTUAL / PROF accept-noop until user/system CPU split lands.

name: getpid
notes: Returns `Process::tgid` (the thread group ID); for processes that haven't done CLONE_THREAD this equals the pid. CLONE_THREAD peers all return the leader's pid. Translated to caller's pid_ns; pid 1 inside a CLONE_NEWPID container, host pid outside.

name: sendfile
notes: Bounded copy through a 4 KiB kernel buffer. Honors the optional `offset_ptr` (when non-NULL, in_fd's position is unchanged and the offset is read/written through the pointer).

name: socket
notes: AF_INET + AF_UNIX (DGRAM via `socketpair` only); SOCK_NONBLOCK / SOCK_CLOEXEC propagated to OpenFile flags.

name: connect
notes: TCP connect parks on the socket's wait queue until smoltcp completes the 3WHS. Iface context picked by destination: loopback addrs (127.x.x.x) route through `loop_iface`; everything else through eth0.

name: accept
notes: Real `accept`: listener has a backlog pool of N smoltcp sockets in `Listen`; accept finds an `Established` slot, swaps it for a fresh listener, returns the connection as a new fd. Honors O_NONBLOCK / parks on the listener's wait queue. Signal-interruptible â€” returns `-EINTR` on a deliverable signal during park.

name: sendto
notes: UDP send (`addr` is the dest); TCP `sendto` ignores `addr` per the ABI.

name: recvfrom
notes: Honors O_NONBLOCK: blocking â†’ park on socket wait queue; non-blocking â†’ -EAGAIN. Signal-interruptible â€” returns `-EINTR` on a deliverable signal during park.

name: sendmsg
notes: Gathers the iovecs into one kernel buffer and writes through the fd (every socket family + ordinary files). `msg_name` / ancillary `SCM_*` data not surfaced.

name: recvmsg
notes: Receives one message and scatters it across the iovecs; honors the fd's blocking mode via the underlying read. Source address / ancillary data reported empty.

name: shutdown
notes: Real half-close. SHUT_RD makes recv return 0 (EOF); SHUT_WR sends FIN via smoltcp `close()` (TCP) and makes send return EPIPE; SHUT_RDWR both. Poll surfaces POLLHUP.

name: bind
notes: A wildcard bind (port 0) on a UDP socket assigns an ephemeral port.

name: listen
notes: Backlog now allocates a real smoltcp listener pool, capped at `MAX_LISTEN_BACKLOG = 16`.

name: getpeername
notes: reports the connected peer's address; -ENOTCONN if not connected. Shares the sockaddr copy-out path with getsockname.

name: socketpair
notes: AF_UNIX SOCK_DGRAM only.

name: setsockopt
notes: Real per-option dispatch on `SockOpts`. Honors `SO_REUSEADDR`/`SO_REUSEPORT`/`SO_KEEPALIVE`/`SO_BROADCAST`/`SO_RCVBUF`/`SO_SNDBUF`/`SO_RCVTIMEO`/`SO_SNDTIMEO`/`SO_LINGER`/`TCP_NODELAY`/`IP_TTL`. Smoltcp-side calls for TCP_NODELAY (Nagle), SO_KEEPALIVE (60s interval), IP_TTL (hop limit). The documented "double the buffer-size on set" rule honored. Unknown options return ENOPROTOOPT.

name: getsockopt
notes: Reads from `SockOpts`. Reports `SO_TYPE` (SOCK_STREAM/DGRAM), `SO_PROTOCOL` (TCP/UDP), `SO_DOMAIN` (AF_INET), `SO_ERROR` (0; no async err yet), and every option `setsockopt` writes.

name: clone
notes: pthread-bundle (`CLONE_VM | CLONE_THREAD | CLONE_FS | CLONE_FILES | CLONE_SIGHAND`) routes to `clone_thread_current` â€” Arc-shared VmSpace + FdTable + sigactions, same tgid, distinct tid. Fork-equivalent flag sets route to fork with deep-copy fds/sigactions. CLONE_PARENT_SETTID / CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID / CLONE_SETTLS honored. CLONE_VFORK parks parent on child's `vfork_done` queue (drained by exec/exit). CLONE_CHILD_SETTID honored for thread case (shared VmSpace); fork case deferred. `CLONE_NEWPID` stages a fresh pid_ns on the parent; the next forked child becomes pid 1 in the new ns. Returned pid + ptid/ctid writes are in the caller's pid_ns.

name: fork
notes: Eager-copy of parent's user-half VmSpace (every leaf user PTE â†’ fresh frame, memcpy bytes, map in child). Child's `TrapFrame` cloned from parent's with `rax=0`; child's first launch IRETQs to parent's syscall return point. fd table is `clone_for_child` (Arc-bump on each `OpenFile`); brk/mmap/sigactions copied.

name: vfork
notes: Forks then parks parent on child's `vfork_done` queue. Drained when child execve()s (via `exec_current`) or exits (every exit path). Shared-VmSpace until execve still deferred (we use a fork-shape copy-on-write VmSpace), but the dominant `vfork+execve` pattern works either way since execve replaces the AS. A sticky `vfork_done_set: AtomicBool` on the child closes a missed-wakeup race when the child execs/exits between fork-return and the parent's enqueue, so a `find | xargs -n1 cmd` pipeline does not wedge on an unreaped vforked child.

name: execve
notes: Tears down current `VmSpace` user half, reloads new ELF, maps fresh user stack, resets brk / mmap arena / sigactions / pending signals / siginfo queue / alt stack / cmdline / exe_path, closes O_CLOEXEC fds, drains `vfork_done`. +x permission check on the binary; S_ISUID and S_ISGID set on the file â†’ euid/suid/fsuid (resp egid/sgid/fsgid) become file owner; ruid/rgid unchanged.

name: exit
notes: Per-CPU scheduler reaps the dying task to `Zombie(code)`. Exit-time futex hygiene: clears the `set_tid_address` futex word + wakes one waiter, walks the `set_robust_list` chain marking each held futex `FUTEX_OWNER_DIED` and waking one waiter. Posts SIGCHLD to parent with `PendingSigInfo { si_code: CLD_EXITED, si_pid: child, si_status }` so a parent under `SA_SIGINFO` sees real values. `wait4` reap drops the `Box<Process>` after releasing GLOBAL â€” `VmSpace::Drop` reclaims the entire user-half page-table tree (leaf frames + L1/L2/L3 + TLB shootdown) and `kernel::futex::drop_vmspace` sweeps any remaining futex queues keyed on the dying VmSpace.

name: wait4
notes: Parent parks on its own `child_exit` `WaitQueue`; `exit_current` / `kill_user_fault` drain it. Status encoded in the documented `wait` status format: normal exit â†’ `(code & 0xff) << 8`, fault-killed â†’ SIGSEGV (low 7 bits). Honors `WNOHANG`. `pid > 0` selects a specific child; `<= 0` selectors are treated as -1 (any) until pgids land. `rusage` ignored. Signal-aware: a deliverable non-SIGCHLD signal during park returns `-EINTR`; SIGCHLD is the wake event itself, the loop just re-checks for a reapable child after each wake. pid arg is in caller's pid_ns and translates to host on entry; reaped pid is snapshotted in caller's pid_ns *before* `drop_chain` destroys the mapping at reap.

name: kill
notes: Full default-action surface plus `kill(-pgid, sig)` process-group fan-out: `pid<0` signals every member of `Pid(-pid)`'s pgrp; `pid==0` fans out to caller's pgrp. Per-signal `default_action()` table (Term / Core / Stop / Cont / Ignore) per `man 7 signal`. SIGKILL / SIGSTOP uncatchable + unblockable (`rt_sigaction` rejects with EINVAL; dispatcher force-defaults). Stop-default signals (SIGSTOP/SIGTSTP/SIGTTIN/SIGTTOU) flip `Process::state` to `Stopped` and notify parent's `wait4(WUNTRACED)`. SIGCONT against Stopped resumes back to Runnable and discards pending Stop-default signals per `man 7 signal`. Term/Core-default signals terminate via `terminate_current_with_signal`, encoding `KilledBySignal { signal }` so `wait4` reports `WIFSIGNALED` (low 7 bits = signum). pid arg is ns-local; positive pid translates to host before signal post; negative pgid translates the abs(pid) ns-local pgid to host before `signal_pgrp`.

name: uname
notes: Six 65-byte fields (sysname/nodename/release/version/machine/domainname). Static values: sysname="Linux", machine="x86_64".

name: shmdt
notes: Walks the calling process's VMAs to find the `VmaBacking::Shm` at `addr`, calls `vm.unmap_keep_frame` for each page (unmaps without freeing â€” frames belong to the segment), removes the VMA, decrements `attached`. If `attached==0 && marked_rmid`, the segment is dropped from the global table.

name: fcntl
notes: `F_DUPFD` / `F_DUPFD_CLOEXEC` / `F_GETFD` / `F_SETFD` / `F_GETFL` / `F_SETFL`. `F_GETLK` / `F_SETLK` / `F_SETLKW` POSIX advisory locks via `kernel::vfs::locks::posix`. SEEK_CUR / SEEK_END whence in `struct flock` honored â€” fd's current offset + inode's size threaded through the fcntl path. **F_OFD_GETLK / F_OFD_SETLK / F_OFD_SETLKW** also wired â€” same advisory-lock semantics but ownership is per-OFD (`Arc<OpenFile>` pointer) so locks survive fork via shared FdTable. F_GET_SEALS / F_ADD_SEALS for memfd.

name: flock
notes: Real BSD whole-file advisory lock. Per-OFD (Arc<OpenFile> ptr) state in a per-inode `FlockState`: Unlocked / Shared(set) / Exclusive(holder). LOCK_NB returns EWOULDBLOCK on conflict; default-blocking parks on per-inode wait queue and retries. Atomic upgrade (sole-SHâ†’EX) and downgrade (EXâ†’SH). OFD close drops the lock automatically via `OpenFile::Drop`.

name: fsync
notes: tmpfs writes are RAM-synchronous; ext4 writes are device-synchronous. No-op success.

name: fdatasync
notes: Same as `fsync`.

name: truncate
notes: Routes to `Inode::truncate`. Invalidates page cache above new length.

name: getdents
notes: Real legacy dirent layout (d_ino + d_off + d_reclen + name + NUL + pad + d_type at offset reclen-1). Distinct from getdents64 (d_type before name). The buffer is correctly shaped for the legacy static binaries that use this syscall.

name: fchdir
notes: Pulls inode from fd table, validates it's a directory, sets `Process::cwd`. Path string is set to "/" as a placeholder (no inodeâ†’path reconstruction yet; real `getcwd` would need a dentry cache).

name: rename
notes: Routes through `renameat2`. Atomic single-FS rename via `Inode::rename` override.

name: mkdir
notes: Routes through `mkdirat(AT_FDCWD)`.

name: rmdir
notes: Routes through `unlinkat(AT_REMOVEDIR)`. Empty-dir check via `Inode::rmdir`.

name: creat
notes: Wraps `openat(AT_FDCWD, path, O_CREAT|O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC, mode)`.

name: link
notes: Routes through `linkat`. Bumps target's nlink via `Inode::bump_nlink`.

name: unlink
notes: Routes through `unlinkat(AT_FDCWD)`. Drops nlink via `Inode::drop_nlink`.

name: symlink
notes: Routes through `symlinkat`. tmpfs creates a Symlink inode storing the target string.

name: readlink
notes: Routes through `readlinkat(AT_FDCWD)`.

name: chmod
notes: Routes through `fchmodat(AT_FDCWD)`. Mode bits stored per-inode (tmpfs).

name: chown
notes: Routes through `fchownat(AT_FDCWD)`. uid/gid stored per-inode (tmpfs).

name: lchown
notes: Same as `chown` (no follow distinction in our resolver yet).

name: umask
notes: Per-process `Process::umask`, default 0o022, inherited across fork.

name: gettimeofday
notes: Wall-clock time via KVM pvclock when available (real seconds-since-1970 from the host); falls back to monotonic-since-boot when pvclock isn't exposed (e.g., a TCG-only environment). NULL `tv` allowed (returns 0). `tz` ignored â€” deprecated by the ABI.

name: getrlimit
notes: RLIMIT_NOFILE=1024 (matches `MAX_FDS`), RLIMIT_STACK=8MiB / unlimited, RLIMIT_AS=128 TiB. Other resources return RLIM_INFINITY.

name: getrusage
notes: Real `ru_utime` from `Process.total_cpu_ns` (charged on every `charge_runtime` from on_tick / yield). `ru_stime` stays 0 â€” user/system CPU split needs syscall-entry/exit instrumentation. RUSAGE_SELF / THREAD honored; RUSAGE_CHILDREN zeroed (per-child accumulation deferred). Memory-pressure fields (`ru_maxrss`, `ru_majflt`, ...) zeroed.

name: sysinfo
notes: Fills the 112-byte `struct sysinfo`. `uptime` from `frame::time::nanos_since_boot()`. `loads[3]` zero (no load-avg sampler). `totalram` / `freeram` from `frame::mm::frame_alloc::stats()`; `sharedram` / `bufferram` zero (no page cache yet). `totalswap` / `freeswap` zero (no swap). `procs` from `sched::process_count_alive()`. `mem_unit = 1` (bytes).

name: times
notes: `tms_utime` real (from `Process.total_cpu_ns / 10ms`); `tms_stime` / `tms_cutime` / `tms_cstime` zeroed (same user/sys-split + per-child gaps as `getrusage`). Returns wall-clock-since-boot in clock_t (100Hz).

name: ptrace
notes: Comprehensive ptrace operation coverage. **Syscall tracing:** TRACEME / ATTACH / DETACH / CONT / SYSCALL / KILL / PEEKDATA / PEEKTEXT / POKEDATA / POKETEXT / GETREGS / SETREGS / SETOPTIONS(TRACESYSGOOD). **Single-step / breakpoints:** SINGLESTEP, int3 â†’ SIGTRAP via IDT trace-trap hook (DPL=3 on #BP gate so user int3 doesn't trigger #GP), POKETEXT into RX (lands via kernel high-half physmap regardless of user PTE flags). **Signal-stop + follow-fork:** signal-stop forwarding (deliver_pending_signals diverts non-SIGKILL signals to a `Signal(sig)` trace stop; tracer suppresses/substitutes/re-delivers via PTRACE_CONT arg), PTRACE_O_TRACEFORK auto-attach (forked children inherit tracer + arm syscall stop), GETEVENTMSG (returns new-child pid for fork events / signal number for signal stops). Cross-VmSpace memory access via `frame::user::peek_other_vmspace` / `poke_other_vmspace`. Test exercises end-to-end TRACEMEâ†’stopâ†’SETOPTIONSâ†’CONTâ†’int3-stopâ†’GETREGSâ†’SYSCALLâ†’write-entry-stopâ†’DETACHâ†’reap. Marker `PTRACE_OK`. Remaining gaps: PTRACE_GETSIGINFO/SETSIGINFO full siginfo_t, TRACEEXEC/TRACECLONE/TRACEVFORK/TRACEEXIT auto-stops, PTRACE_GET_SYSCALL_INFO fast path, hardware watchpoints DR0-DR7.

name: getuid
notes: Real credential model. Returns `Process::creds::ruid`. Boot starts as root (uid=0); `setresuid` etc. drop privileges with strict transition validation.

name: syslog
notes: Kernel log ring buffer (64 KiB, the documented `LOG_BUF_SHIFT = 16` size). Mirrors every byte from `frame::println!` via a function-pointer sink wired in `kernel::init`. SYSLOG_ACTION_READ / READ_ALL / READ_CLEAR copy bytes to userspace; CLEAR / OPEN / CLOSE / CONSOLE_* are no-ops or in-ring state changes; SIZE_UNREAD / SIZE_BUFFER report ring stats. User-space readers retrieve the boot log via SYSLOG_ACTION_READ / READ_ALL / READ_CLEAR.

name: getgid
notes: Returns `Process::creds::rgid`.

name: setuid
notes: POSIX simple-form privilege drop. Privileged caller (euid==0): full drop (ruid=euid=suid=uid). Unprivileged caller: `uid` must be one of (ruid, euid, suid); only `euid` changes (so suid keeps the saved root copy for setuid binaries). Returns -EPERM otherwise.

name: setgid
notes: Same shape as `setuid` for group ids.

name: geteuid
notes: Returns `Process::creds::euid`.

name: getegid
notes: Returns `Process::creds::egid`.

name: setpgid
notes: Real `Process::pgid` field + helper in `kernel::sched`. Validates target in caller's session, target not a session leader (`EPERM` otherwise). `pid==0` â†” caller; `pgid==0` â†” target's own pid. Both args are ns-local; translated to host pids before storing.

name: getppid
notes: Returns parent pid; 0 for boot's first user task. Translates parent's host pid to caller's pid_ns view. Returns 0 when parent isn't visible from caller's pid_ns (the "parent in a different ns" semantic).

name: getpgrp
notes: Returns the calling task's `pgid` (the job-control group it belongs to). pgid translated to caller's pid_ns view.

name: setsid
notes: Caller becomes session leader: `sid = pgid = pid`. Returns `EPERM` if caller is already a process-group leader. Returned new sid translated to caller's pid_ns view.

name: setreuid
notes: The legacy two-arg form. Same transition validation as `setresuid`; additionally updates `suid` if either field changes (so subsequent `setuid` retains a saved-root fallback).

name: setregid
notes: Same shape on group ids.

name: getgroups
notes: Returns the real supplementary group list from `Process::creds::groups`. `size==0` is the probe-for-count form.

name: setgroups
notes: Privileged-only (CAP_SETGID). Replaces the supplementary group list. Cap at 256 entries. -EPERM if non-privileged.

name: setresuid
notes: Strict transition rule: each new (ruid, euid, suid) value must equal one of the current triple, OR caller is privileged (euid==0). `(uid_t)-1` â†” leave unchanged. Returns -EPERM on disallowed transition.

name: getresuid
notes: Returns the real (ruid, euid, suid) triple from `Process::creds`. NULL pointer â†” skip.

name: setresgid
notes: Same shape as `setresuid` for group ids.

name: getresgid
notes: Real (rgid, egid, sgid).

name: getpgid
notes: Read `Process::pgid` of the named pid (or caller if `pid==0`). Input pid + return pgid both translated through caller's pid_ns.

name: setfsuid
notes: Filesystem uid override. Returns the OLD `fsuid`. Sets new fsuid only if value is one of (ruid, euid, suid, fsuid) or caller is privileged.

name: setfsgid
notes: Same shape on group ids.

name: getsid
notes: Read `Process::sid`. Input pid + return sid both translated through caller's pid_ns.

name: capget
notes: The v3 (0x20080522) capability layout. Returns target's effective/permitted/inheritable caps packed as two `__user_cap_data_struct` slots. Wrong version â†’ EINVAL with kernel's preferred version written back.

name: capset
notes: Self-only (cross-process â†’ EPERM). Validation: new_perm âŠ† old_perm; new_eff âŠ† new_perm; new_inh âŠ† (old_inh âˆª old_perm); new_inh âŠ† (caps_bnd âˆª old_inh).

name: rt_sigpending
notes: Copies `Process.pending_signals` (u64 bitmask) to user. Validates `sigsetsize == 8` (x86_64 sigset_t).

name: rt_sigtimedwait
notes: Synchronous signal wait. Pre-checks pending against `set_mask`, consumes + returns on hit. Otherwise: NULL timeout â†’ block forever; zero timespec â†’ poll-only (EAGAIN if nothing pending); finite timespec â†’ register a `crate::timeout` deadline-park, sleep until signal-or-deadline, return EAGAIN on timeout expiry.

name: rt_sigqueueinfo
notes: Like `kill(pid, sig)` but copies the user-supplied 128-byte siginfo_t. The positive-si_code-from-unprivileged check enforced (-EPERM unless euid==0). Routes through existing `send_signal_with_info`.

name: rt_sigsuspend
notes: Atomically replace blocked-signal mask, park on `sleep_until_signal` (new helper that loops on signal_wait), restore original mask on signal arrival, return -EINTR. SIGKILL/SIGSTOP can't be blocked.

name: sigaltstack
notes: Stores per-process; refuses to change while running on the alt stack (EPERM); cleared on `execve`.

name: mknod
notes: Routes through `mknodat(AT_FDCWD)`. Decodes kind from `mode & S_IFMT`.

name: uselib
notes: DEPRECATED: obsolete shared-library loader. Returns `-ENOSYS` per the documented ABI for this deprecated entry point. Loaded shared libraries directly (pre-`ld.so` machinery); modern userland uses the dynamic linker.

name: personality
notes: Returns `0` (the base personality, value 0) for any input. We don't run other personalities.

name: ustat
notes: `-ENOSYS`. Deprecated; replaced by `statfs(2)`. Userland routes `ustat` through statfs; binaries probing the syscall directly fall back gracefully.

name: statfs
notes: 120-byte `struct statfs64`. Magic 0x01021994 for tmpfs, 0xEF53 for ext4.

name: sysfs
notes: `-ENOSYS`. Distinct from the `/sys` directory tree. The syscall returned filesystem-type names by index; deprecated in favor of `/proc/filesystems`. Modern kernels mostly return -ENOSYS.

name: getpriority
notes: Reads `Process::nice`, returns `20 - nice` (the encoded form). `which == PRIO_PROCESS` only; PRIO_PGRP / PRIO_USER return EINVAL.

name: setpriority
notes: Clamps to `[-20, 19]`, writes `Process::nice`. Real round-trip with getpriority. CAP_SYS_NICE for negative values not enforced. Advisory until the scheduler honors nice for time-slicing.

name: sched_setparam
notes: Change RT priority of an already-RT task without changing policy. For non-RT tasks the only valid value is 0; otherwise `-EINVAL`. CAP_SYS_NICE gated.

name: sched_getparam
notes: Fills `sched_priority` with the task's RT priority (0 for non-RT).

name: sched_setscheduler
notes: Switches a task between SCHED_OTHER (Cfs) / SCHED_FIFO / SCHED_RR / SCHED_BATCH / SCHED_IDLE. RT priority `[1, 99]`; OTHER/BATCH/IDLE require priority==0. CAP_SYS_NICE gates RT entry. `set_sched_class` re-routes the task between class sub-queues atomically. SCHED_DEADLINE rejected here â€” must use `sched_setattr`.

name: sched_getscheduler
notes: Returns 0 (OTHER), 1 (FIFO), 2 (RR), 6 (DEADLINE).

name: sched_get_priority_max
notes: 99 for FIFO/RR; 0 for OTHER/BATCH/IDLE.

name: sched_get_priority_min
notes: 1 for FIFO/RR; 0 for OTHER/BATCH/IDLE.

name: sched_rr_get_interval
notes: `SCHED_RR_TIMESLICE_NS` (100 ms) for RR tasks, zeroed for everything else.

name: mlock
notes: Validates the address range covers existing VMAs (-ENOMEM on holes), returns success. We have no swap, so every page is RAM-resident by construction â€” the "page is locked in physical memory" semantic is automatic on a no-swap kernel.

name: munlock
notes: Symmetric to `mlock`.

name: mlockall
notes: Validates `flags` is one of MCL_CURRENT/MCL_FUTURE/MCL_ONFAULT (with the 4.4-era ONFAULT bit), returns success.

name: munlockall
notes: Always 0.

name: vhangup
notes: Root: 0 (no controlling-tty teardown to do â€” single-console kernel). Non-root: -EPERM. CAP_SYS_TTY_CONFIG is the documented requirement; we use the simpler euid==0 check.

name: modify_ldt
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: 32-bit not supported. `-ENOSYS`. x86_64 doesn't use a per-task LDT (GDT-only segment model). The x86_64 ABI returns -ENOSYS for this i386-era call.

name: pivot_root
notes: Real semantics on top of the per-process VFS context. Mounts the old root at `put_old`'s inode-id via `vfs::mount_install`, then sets `Process::fs_root` to `new_root`. Subsequent path resolution walks from new_root; old root reachable via `/<put_old>`.

name: _sysctl
notes: DEAD: removed; returns `-ENOSYS`. Replaced by writes to `/proc/sys/`.

name: prctl
notes: Options honored: `PR_{SET,GET}_NAME`, `PR_{SET,GET}_NO_NEW_PRIVS`, `PR_{SET,GET}_SECCOMP`, `PR_CAPBSET_READ`/`_DROP`, `PR_{SET,GET}_KEEPCAPS`, `PR_{SET,GET}_DUMPABLE`. `PR_{SET,GET}_CHILD_SUBREAPER` + `PR_{SET,GET}_PDEATHSIG` are now real â€” `exit_current` runs `handle_dying_children` which (1) for each child with `pdeathsig` set, posts that signal to the child; (2) reparents each child by walking the dying task's parent chain looking for the nearest live ancestor with `child_subreaper.load() == true`, falling back to pid 1. Anything else: `-EINVAL`.

name: arch_prctl
notes: `ARCH_SET_FS` writes `IA32_FS_BASE` MSR + records on `Process`; `ARCH_GET_FS` reads back. The scheduler restores `proc.fs_base` to the MSR on every task switch (`switch_to_pid`), so per-process TLS survives context switches. GS reserved for kernel per-CPU storage â€” `ARCH_SET_GS` / `ARCH_GET_GS` return EINVAL.

name: adjtimex
notes: Reads/writes the 184-byte `struct timex`. `modes==0`: pure read (no permission check); fills `time` with current wall clock, `tick=10000` (100Hz), `precision=1`. `modes!=0`: requires euid==0 (CAP_SYS_TIME). `ADJ_SETOFFSET` applies a one-shot offset jump to `frame::cpu::clock`'s `WALL_CLOCK_OFFSET_NS`. `ADJ_NANO` modifier honored (interpret `time.tv_*` as nsec vs default usec). Other NTP-slewing modes (ADJ_OFFSET / FREQUENCY / MAXERROR / ESTERROR / STATUS / TIMECONST / TICK) are accepted-with-EPERM-gate but don't apply slewing â€” pvclock owns wall time, kernel TSC owns monotonic, no NTP daemon needed. Returns TIME_OK (0).

name: setrlimit
notes: Wraps `prlimit64(self, resource, new_rlim, NULL)`. The full credential / hardness checks already live in prlimit64; this is the legacy single-process variant.

name: chroot
notes: Per-process root override. `Process::fs_root` set via `sched::set_current_fs_root`; consulted by `vfs::path::Context::current()` at every `resolve` call. Inherited across fork; preserved across execve. No capability check (single-user kernel; CAP_SYS_CHROOT is the documented requirement).

name: sync
notes: No-op success.

name: acct
notes: `-EPERM`. BSD process accounting requires CAP_SYS_PACCT and a designated accounting file; we don't model the subsystem. The documented ABI returns -EPERM before doing anything when the cap check fails.

name: settimeofday
notes: Wraps `frame::cpu::clock::set_wall_clock_target`. CAP_SYS_TIME (= euid==0) required; -EPERM otherwise. `tz` ignored (deprecated). NULL `tv` â†’ 0. Validates usec range.

name: mount
notes: tmpfs + cgroup2 + proc + sys + ext4 (loopback). Mount table per-process + per-mount-ns. MS_REMOUNT, MS_BIND, MS_MOVE, propagation flags (PRIVATE/SHARED/SLAVE/UNBINDABLE) all real. Fs types not in our table reject with ENODEV â€” the documented behavior for absent fs types.

name: umount2
notes: Removes mount table entry for the target dir's inode_id.

name: swapon
notes: In scope; the swap subsystem is planned after v0.0.1. Returns -ENOSYS (not yet implemented) until then.

name: swapoff
notes: Same as swapon (#167): in scope, planned after v0.0.1, -ENOSYS until built.

name: reboot
notes: Magic1/magic2 handshake validated; CAP_SYS_BOOT gated. CAD_OFF/CAD_ON return 0. HALT/POWER_OFF/RESTART halt the VM via `frame::io::qemu_exit::exit(Success)` (the same shutdown the test harness uses).

name: sethostname
notes: Per-process UTS namespace. Writes go to the calling proc's UTS ns. CAP_SYS_ADMIN gate.

name: setdomainname
notes: Same shape as sethostname.

name: iopl
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: ring0 unsafe. `-EPERM`. Direct port-I/O privilege; this is a VM kernel and doesn't grant ring-3 port access. The ABI returns -EPERM without CAP_SYS_RAWIO.

name: ioperm
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: ring0 unsafe. Same as `iopl` (#172).

name: create_module
notes: DEAD: removed; returns `-ENOSYS`. Module-loading API.

name: init_module
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: ring0 unsafe. `-ENOSYS`. We don't support loadable kernel modules.

name: delete_module
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: ring0 unsafe. `-ENOSYS`. Same as `init_module` (#175).

name: get_kernel_syms
notes: DEAD: removed; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: query_module
notes: DEAD: removed; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: nfsservctl
notes: DEAD: removed; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: getpmsg
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: putpmsg
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`. Same as `getpmsg` (#181).

name: afs_syscall
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: tuxcall
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: security
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: gettid
notes: Returns `current_pid()` (the per-task TID). For processes that haven't done CLONE_THREAD this equals tgid (and `getpid()`); for thread peers it differs. Translated to caller's pid_ns view.

name: readahead
notes: Validates fd is a regular file (-EINVAL otherwise), returns 0. POSIX hint to populate the page cache; we don't yet do active prefetch. The ABI returns 0 even when the underlying fs/cache decides not to read ahead, so this matches semantics.

name: setxattr
notes: Per-inode xattr table on tmpfs. Honors XATTR_CREATE / XATTR_REPLACE.

name: getxattr
notes: Size-probe shape (`size==0`) returns the value's length without copying. Returns `ERANGE` on undersized buf.

name: listxattr
notes: NUL-separated, NUL-terminated name list. Size probe shape supported.

name: tkill
notes: Legacy single-thread kill. tid arg â†’ host pid via caller's pid_ns; `send_signal_with_info` with SI_TKILL; sender pid encoded in target's pid_ns view.

name: time
notes: Returns `wall_clock_nanos / 1e9`; writes the same value through `t_ptr` if non-NULL.

name: futex
notes: `FUTEX_WAIT` / `WAKE`, `WAIT_BITSET` / `WAKE_BITSET` / `REQUEUE` / `CMP_REQUEUE` / `WAKE_OP`. Real timeouts via `kernel::timeout` deadline-park â€” `FUTEX_WAIT` with non-NULL `timespec` returns `-ETIMEDOUT` when the deadline fires before a wake. Key is `(vmspace_id, vaddr)`. Signal-interruptible (returns `-EINTR`). `VmSpace::Drop` sweeps dead-vmspace entries. `FUTEX_LOCK_PI` (#6) / `FUTEX_UNLOCK_PI` (#7) / `FUTEX_TRYLOCK_PI` (#8) / `FUTEX_LOCK_PI2` (#13). Real PI mutex with priority-boost via temporary class swap, chain walk through `pi_blocked_on` bounded by `MAX_PI_CHAIN_DEPTH = 16` (cycle â†’ `-EDEADLK`), atomic `cmpxchg_user_u32` + `atomic_or_user_u32` (lock-prefixed x86 asm in `frame::user`) to maintain TID + `FUTEX_WAITERS` + `FUTEX_OWNER_DIED` bits in lockstep with userland's fastpath CAS. Owner-died on `exit_current` sets the bit + wakes the top waiter for userland's robust-mutex recovery. Self-deadlock check returns `-EDEADLK`. `FUTEX_TID_MASK` carries ns-local pid (matches gettid). Pid translation at the user-memory boundary. `FUTEX_WAIT_REQUEUE_PI` (#11) / `FUTEX_CMP_REQUEUE_PI` (#12) â€” PI condvars (pthread_cond_t + PRIO_INHERIT mutex). `wait_requeue_pi` parks on the condvar's address with `pi_blocked_on=Some(mutex_key)`; `cmp_requeue_pi` wakes one (transferring mutex ownership), requeues rest onto the mutex's PI tree. proc_pi_futex test verifies all six round-trips.

name: sched_setaffinity
notes: Accept-noop, the documented "hint not a contract" semantic. The scheduler picks `home_cpu` at task spawn and round-robins; explicit CPU pinning would need cpuset-cgroup support. Conformant per `man 2 sched_setaffinity`: kernels that don't honor the hint must still accept the call.

name: sched_getaffinity
notes: Returns a `MAX_CPUS`-bit mask of all online CPUs. The advertised mask is honest given that we don't restrict CPU placement.

name: set_thread_area
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: 32-bit not supported. Returns -ENOSYS â€” the canonical x86_64 answer. This is i386's TLS API (writes a GDT entry); on x86_64 the equivalent is `arch_prctl(ARCH_SET_FS, addr)`. The x86_64 ABI returns -ENOSYS here.

name: get_thread_area
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: 32-bit not supported. Same as `set_thread_area` (#205) â€” returns -ENOSYS, x86_64-canonical.

name: lookup_dcookie
notes: DEAD: removed; returns `-ENOSYS`. The binary dcookie API.

name: epoll_create
notes: Wraps `epoll_create1(0)`. The legacy `size` arg is ignored â€” kept only for ABI compat. We reject `size <= 0` (-EINVAL) per the legacy ABI.

name: epoll_ctl_old
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: epoll_wait_old
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`. Same as `epoll_ctl_old` (#214).

name: set_tid_address
notes: Stores user pointer; returns calling tid. At process exit, `kernel::futex::clear_child_tid` writes 0 to the registered address and wakes one waiter (a userland `pthread_join` uses this to detect thread death). Return value is in caller's pid_ns.

name: restart_syscall
notes: `-ENOSYS`. Internal kernel mechanism for SA_RESTART signal handling â€” userspace shouldn't call this directly. The ABI returns -ENOSYS to userspace callers; the actual restart logic happens kernel-side via tail-call.

name: fadvise64
notes: Accept and ignore. fadvise is advisory and a no-op for `POSIX_FADV_NORMAL` / `RANDOM` / `SEQUENTIAL` / `NOREUSE`; `WILLNEED` would benefit from readahead we don't have; `DONTNEED` already happens on close via the page cache.

name: timer_create
notes: Per-process `timer_t` table (`Process.timers`). Accepts CLOCK_REALTIME / CLOCK_MONOTONIC / CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW / CLOCK_BOOTTIME. Parses `struct sigevent`: SIGEV_SIGNAL (queued signal), SIGEV_NONE (counts, no delivery), SIGEV_THREAD (treated as SIGEV_SIGNAL â€” the helper-thread is a userspace librt detail), SIGEV_THREAD_ID (validates the target thread). Returns an allocated `timer_t` to `timer_id`. NULL `sevp` defaults to SIGALRM.

name: timer_settime
notes: Arm/disarm door (cancel-then-register on `timeout::register_callback`, keyed per-timer). Reads `struct itimerspec` (initial + interval); `it_value==0` disarms. TIMER_ABSTIME converts the absolute deadline against the timer's clock. Returns the prior interval + remaining in `old_value`. Resets overrun on re-arm.

name: timer_gettime
notes: Reads the timer's interval and remaining time until the next expiry into `struct itimerspec`; reports zeros when disarmed.

name: timer_getoverrun
notes: Returns the overrun count of the most recently delivered expiry (missed periods since the last delivery); reset each delivery.

name: timer_delete
notes: Disarms (cancels the pending callback) and frees the `timer_t` slot. Cleared en masse on execve and process exit.

name: clock_settime
notes: Accepts CLOCK_REALTIME / CLOCK_REALTIME_COARSE (operator-installed offset). MONOTONIC* and CPU-time clocks return -EINVAL (monotonic can't be set, CPU clocks are read-only). Computes target_ns = sec * 1e9 + nsec; calls `set_wall_clock_target`. CAP_SYS_TIME (= euid==0) required.

name: clock_gettime
notes: TSC frequency calibrated from KVM pvclock or CPUID 0x15/0x16 at boot (`frame::cpu::clock::init`). REALTIME / REALTIME_COARSE â†’ KVM-pvclock wall time when available (real seconds-since-1970), else monotonic-since-boot fallback. MONOTONIC / MONOTONIC_RAW / MONOTONIC_COARSE / BOOTTIME â†’ TSC-derived nanos-since-boot. PROCESS_CPUTIME / THREAD_CPUTIME â†’ 0 (no per-task CPU accounting yet). Unknown clock ids â†’ EINVAL.

name: clock_getres
notes: 1 ns for the fine clocks (REALTIME / MONOTONIC / MONOTONIC_RAW / BOOTTIME); 10 ms for the COARSE variants (matches our 100 Hz scheduler tick). NULL `res` accepted.

name: clock_nanosleep
notes: Validates clockid (CLOCK_REALTIME/MONOTONIC/BOOTTIME accepted; CPU-time clocks return -EINVAL), validates `flags` (0 or TIMER_ABSTIME), parks via `sched::sleep_until` against the computed deadline. On signal-interrupt fills `remain` (relative-mode only) and returns -EINTR.

name: exit_group
notes: Real `exit_group_current` â€” pre-zombifies every Process whose `tgid` matches the calling task's, then routes the calling thread through `exit_current` (preserving the SIGCHLD-to-parent path). Terminates the entire thread group, unlike `exit` which terminates only the calling thread.

name: epoll_wait
notes: Real blocking â€” parks on every registered inode's wait queue, wakes via `Inode::for_each_wait_queue`. A real blocking wait, not a byte-eating probe.

name: epoll_ctl
notes: ADD/MOD/DEL.

name: tgkill
notes: tid arg is ns-local; translated to host before signal post. Sender pid in siginfo (`si_pid`) is translated into the *target's* pid_ns view (si_pid is in the receiver's ns). Sets `si_code = SI_TKILL`.

name: utimes
notes: Sets atime + mtime from a `struct timeval[2]` (microsecond precision, converted to nanoseconds) on the named path, or both to the current time when `times` is NULL. Shares the path-resolution and `Inode::set_times` core with `utimensat`; resolves against the current working directory. No UTIME_NOW / UTIME_OMIT (those are `utimensat`-only).

name: vserver
notes: DEAD: reserved syscall slot, never implemented; returns `-ENOSYS`.

name: mbind
notes: Single-NUMA correct: validates `mode` is one of MPOL_DEFAULT/PREFERRED/BIND/INTERLEAVE/LOCAL; if a nodemask is supplied, validates only node 0 is requested (-EINVAL otherwise). On a 1-node system this collapses to "MPOL_DEFAULT-equivalent succeeds", which matches the documented behavior.

name: set_mempolicy
notes: Same single-NUMA validation as `mbind` (#237).

name: get_mempolicy
notes: Reports MPOL_DEFAULT + nodemask = 0b1 (only node 0 set). Honors MPOL_F_NODE / MPOL_F_ADDR flags.

name: kexec_load
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: ring0 unsafe. `-EPERM`. Loading a new kernel image to boot into; we don't support kexec. The documented ABI requires CAP_SYS_BOOT.

name: waitid
notes: Shares wait4_current; populates user siginfo_t with (CLD_EXITED/CLD_KILLED, si_pid, si_status). id arg ns-local â†’ host on entry; siginfo `si_pid` is the reaped pid in the caller's pid_ns view.

name: add_key
notes: Returns the documented `-EOPNOTSUPP` when kernel key-retention is unavailable. Userland detects this and takes the no-keyring path.

name: request_key
notes: Same as `add_key` â€” `-EOPNOTSUPP`.

name: keyctl
notes: KEYCTL_JOIN_SESSION_KEYRING / KEYCTL_GET_KEYRING_ID return serial 1 so userland's startup probe satisfies. KEYCTL_DESCRIBE returns ENOKEY. Anything else returns `-EOPNOTSUPP`. The shape userland sees is consistent with key-retention being unavailable.

name: migrate_pages
notes: `-ENOSYS`. Move pages between NUMA nodes; we have one node, nothing to migrate.

name: openat
notes: `O_CREAT/O_TRUNC/O_APPEND` + the path resolver. Real permission check via `Credentials::can_access` â€” non-root callers blocked from opening files whose mode bits don't permit r/w as requested.

name: mkdirat
notes: tmpfs only. `mkdir("/")` returns EEXIST, so `mkdir -p` works â€” its parent-walk loop uses EEXIST as the stop signal.

name: mknodat
notes: `mknod(2)` routes through `mknodat(AT_FDCWD)`. Decodes kind from `mode & S_IFMT`; rdev provides major/minor for char/block.

name: futimesat
notes: Sets atime + mtime from a `struct timeval[2]` (microsecond precision, converted to nanoseconds) on a path resolved relative to `dirfd`, or both to the current time when `times` is NULL. Shares the path-resolution and `Inode::set_times` core with `utimensat`. No UTIME_NOW / UTIME_OMIT (those are `utimensat`-only).

name: renameat
notes: tmpfs only.

name: linkat
notes: tmpfs only.

name: symlinkat
notes: tmpfs only.

name: readlinkat
notes: tmpfs only.

name: pselect6
notes: Like `select` but a `timespec` timeout. The 6th arg (sigmask) is not yet applied â€” callers needing the atomic mask swap aren't covered; most pass NULL. Shares the `poll_wait` core + fd_set marshalling with `select`.

name: ppoll
notes: `poll` with a `timespec` deadline. sigmask not applied (see pselect6). Shares the `poll_wait` core with `poll`.

name: unshare
notes: `CLONE_NEWNS` real (snapshot mount table). `CLONE_NEWUSER` real (UserNamespace alloc). `CLONE_NEWUTS` real (UtsNamespace + hostname). `CLONE_NEWPID` real (PID ns â€” next forked child becomes pid 1). `CLONE_NEWIPC` / `CLONE_NEWCGROUP` / `CLONE_NEWTIME` allocate the per-process slot â€” userland tooling that probes for ns membership sees a distinct ns id. CLONE_NEWTIME is marker-only (the time-base is a per-task offset, only adjusted via `/proc/self/timens_offsets`); we match that shape. `CLONE_NEWNET` returns `-EINVAL` honestly (no per-ns network stack â€” the smoltcp-stack is global).

name: set_robust_list
notes: Stores user-vaddr head; at exit `kernel::futex::exit_robust_list` walks the 64-bit ABI robust-futex list (head: `list / futex_offset / list_op_pending`; entries: linked `robust_list { next }`; futex addr = `entry + futex_offset`; cap = `ROBUST_LIST_LIMIT = 2048`). For each live entry sets `FUTEX_OWNER_DIED` on the futex word and wakes one waiter, per the documented robust-futex exit contract.

name: splice
notes: Buffered shovel (4 KiB chunks) routes through `copy_file_range` core. Functionally equivalent at the ABI; the only difference is performance (true zero-copy page-stealing between pipe buffers vs. a copy here). Userland sees identical semantics â€” same return value, same error codes, same data movement.

name: tee
notes: Real no-consume semantics via new `Inode::peek_at` (Pipe override copies front-of-ring without popping). Source pipe still drainable by the original consumer.

name: sync_file_range
notes: No-op success.

name: vmsplice
notes: Direction inferred from the pipe end's open flags. `is_writable()` â†’ routes to `writev` (gather user iovs into pipe); else routes to `readv` (scatter pipe contents into user iovs).

name: move_pages
notes: `-ENOSYS`. Same as `migrate_pages` (#256); single-NUMA.

name: utimensat
notes: Decodes UTIME_NOW / UTIME_OMIT, dispatches through `Inode::set_times`.

name: epoll_pwait
notes: `epoll_wait` + per-call sigmask. Atomic sigmask swap: the supplied 8-byte sigmask is installed on `Process.blocked_signals` for the wait duration, restored on return. `sigsetsize == 8` enforced (x86_64 sigset_t shape).

name: timerfd_create
notes: `TimerFdInode` Inode-trait. CLOCK_REALTIME/MONOTONIC accepted (both monotonic-shape). TFD_NONBLOCK / TFD_CLOEXEC.

name: eventfd
notes: Wraps `eventfd2(initval, 0)`. Legacy nameless variant.

name: fallocate
notes: Default mode + `FALLOC_FL_KEEP_SIZE`: extend file (truncate-up). `FALLOC_FL_PUNCH_HOLE` (zero the byte range; requires KEEP_SIZE) + `FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE` (zero range, optionally extend) â€” both real via repeated `inode.write_at(zeros)`. COLLAPSE_RANGE / INSERT_RANGE / UNSHARE_RANGE not supported.

name: timerfd_settime
notes: Reads `struct itimerspec`; arms via callback-driven `timeout::register_callback`. TFD_TIMER_ABSTIME honored.

name: timerfd_gettime
notes: Writes current `it_interval` + remaining `it_value` (= deadline âˆ’ now).

name: accept4
notes: Same backing as `accept`. Flags arg ignored; SOCK_NONBLOCK on the new fd is not yet propagated.

name: signalfd4
notes: Real `Inode`-backed signalfd. Reads pending-and-not-yet-delivered signals matching the subscription mask, returns 128-byte `signalfd_siginfo` records. Wake on signal post via per-process `signalfd_waiters` queue.

name: eventfd2
notes: `EventFdInode` Inode-trait: u64 counter, blocking read/write, EFD_SEMAPHORE/EFD_NONBLOCK/EFD_CLOEXEC. Per-eventfd wait queue wakes blocked pollers / readers / writers.

name: epoll_create1
notes: `EPOLL_CLOEXEC` honored.

name: pipe2
notes: `O_NONBLOCK/O_CLOEXEC`.

name: preadv
notes: Gather-read at offset. Unpacks `(offset_lo | offset_hi << 32)` per the syscall ABI, walks iovecs calling `pread64` on each with the cursor.

name: pwritev
notes: Gather-write at offset. Same shape as `preadv`, calls `pwrite64`.

name: rt_tgsigqueueinfo
notes: Like `rt_sigqueueinfo` (#129) but per-thread: validates the named tid is in the named tgid before delivering. Same EPERM check on positive si_code.

name: prlimit64
notes: Per-process `rlimits: [Option<Rlimit>; 16]` on `Process`. Reads return the stored rlimit or the kernel default. Writes accept and store `new_rlim` (rejecting `cur > max`). `pid` must be 0 or the calling pid (cross-process rlimit needs a credentials/capability model we don't yet have). `RLIMIT_NOFILE` (resource 7) writes plumb through to the calling process's `FdTable::set_soft_cap` â€” `install` / `dup` honor the new cap on every alloc; `new.max` clamped to `HARD_MAX_FDS = 65 536`.

name: clock_adjtime
notes: Per-clock variant of `adjtimex` (#159). Accepts CLOCK_REALTIME / CLOCK_REALTIME_COARSE; other clocks return -EINVAL (most clocks reject adjustment per the ABI). Routes through `sys_adjtimex`.

name: getcpu
notes: Returns the calling task's current CPU (from `frame::cpu::per_cpu::current_cpu_id`) and NUMA node (always 0; we're single-NUMA). Legacy `tcache` arg ignored.

name: process_vm_readv
notes: Cross-process scatter/gather memory read. Translates pid from caller's pid_ns; enforces ptrace_may_attach (root or matching uid + dumpable); parses local + remote iovecs; bounces bytes through a 4 KiB tmp buffer using `frame::user::peek_other_vmspace` (same primitive ptrace's PEEKDATA uses). Returns total bytes copied.

name: process_vm_writev
notes: Symmetric to `process_vm_readv`. Uses `poke_other_vmspace`.

name: finit_module
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: ring0 unsafe. `-ENOSYS`. Same as `init_module` (#175); we don't load kernel modules.

name: sched_setattr
notes: Accepts `struct sched_attr` (size + policy + nice + priority + runtime + deadline + period). For SCHED_DEADLINE: validates `runtime>0 && deadline>0 && period>0 && runtime <= deadline <= period`. Per-CPU bandwidth admission via `dl_total_bw` cap at `DL_BW_MAX = 950_000` (95%). Real CBS enforcement â€” `on_tick` decrements `dl_runtime_remaining`, transitions to `DlThrottled` at zero, replenishes via `dl_replenish_callback` registered with `crate::timeout`. CAP_SYS_NICE gates RT/DL entry.

name: sched_getattr
notes: Fills `struct sched_attr` with the target's policy + nice + priority + (runtime, deadline, period) for DEADLINE.

name: renameat2
notes: RENAME_NOREPLACE / RENAME_EXCHANGE / RENAME_WHITEOUT flags accepted-and-ignored; default replace-on-collision behavior.

name: seccomp
notes: Real cBPF interpreter + verifier; SECCOMP_SET_MODE_STRICT and SECCOMP_SET_MODE_FILTER. Per-process filter chain (Vec<Arc<BpfProgram>>). Hook in syscall::dispatch evaluates chain on every entry. Actions: ALLOW / ERRNO / TRAP (SIGSYS) / KILL_THREAD / KILL_PROCESS / LOG synchronously dispatched; KILL is synchronous (terminate before sysret). Requires PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS or CAP_SYS_ADMIN.

name: getrandom
notes: Backed by virtio-rng (`virtio::fill_random`). `GRND_*` flags accepted but ignored. Returns EAGAIN if virtio-rng is unavailable.

name: memfd_create
notes: Anonymous RAM-backed fd. Allocates a fresh `TmpfsInode::new_file()` (no directory attachment), wraps in `OpenFile::new(â€¦, RDWR)`, installs in caller's fds. read/write/lseek/ftruncate already work via the tmpfs Inode trait. `MFD_CLOEXEC` honored; `MFD_ALLOW_SEALING` / `MFD_NOEXEC_SEAL` / `MFD_EXEC` accept-noop (seals not modeled); `MFD_HUGETLB` rejected with EINVAL. Used for malloc arena growth and dlopen page-sharing.

name: kexec_file_load
notes: OUT-OF-SCOPE: ring0 unsafe. `-EPERM`. Same as `kexec_load` (#246); we don't kexec.

name: execveat
notes: execveat(dirfd, pathname, argv, envp, flags); same image-replacement path as execve, with dirfd / AT_EMPTY_PATH resolution.

name: membarrier
notes: MEMBARRIER_CMD_QUERY returns the supported-cmd bitmask. GLOBAL / GLOBAL_EXPEDITED / PRIVATE_EXPEDITED / PRIVATE_EXPEDITED_SYNC_CORE issue a SeqCst fence (sufficient on x86_64's TSO model â€” an `mfence`). REGISTER_* commands are no-op success because x86_64 expedited paths don't need pre-registration. Unknown cmds return -EINVAL.

name: mlock2
notes: Same shape as `mlock` (#149) with a `flags` arg accepting MLOCK_ONFAULT (1). Validates flags + range.

name: copy_file_range
notes: Kernel-side copy between two regular files. 4 KiB-chunk shovel; honors optional `loff_t *` for both sides (NULL â†” use the fd's current position).

name: preadv2
notes: `preadv` plus an unused `flags` argument (`RWF_HIPRI`, `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_NOWAIT`). Flags ignored; same shape otherwise.

name: pwritev2
notes: `pwritev` with the same flag-ignore note.

name: pkey_mprotect
notes: Returns -EOPNOTSUPP. The documented ABI requires CPUID.7.ECX:bit3 (PKU / Memory Protection Keys) to be set; our QEMU `-cpu` model doesn't expose it. The honest answer is "this hardware doesn't support MPK" â€” the documented response is -EOPNOTSUPP from pkey_alloc on CPUs without PKU, with pkey_mprotect/pkey_free moot.

name: pkey_alloc
notes: Same as `pkey_mprotect` (#329).

name: pkey_free
notes: Same as `pkey_mprotect` (#329).

name: statx
notes: 256-byte `struct statx`. AT_EMPTY_PATH supported.

name: rseq
notes: Registration + per-task storage + CPU-id seed at register time. Validates `rseq_len âˆˆ {32,36,40}`, 32-byte alignment, `RSEQ_FLAG_UNREGISTER`. cpu_id is updated at every context switch (`switch_to_pid` writes the new home_cpu's id into the registered rseq area before resuming user mode). Critical-section abort-on-preempt path remains opt-in for the few workloads that use it explicitly â€” we map the abort handler at restart_ip if the trap occurred inside the registered cs region. Per-CPU malloc / tcache caching works end-to-end.

name: pidfd_send_signal
notes: Routes through pidfd's `inode-ptr â†’ Pid` index, applies the same `Credentials::can_signal` check as `kill(2)`, then `send_signal`. `_info` (siginfo) ignored â€” synthesizes SI_USER. flags must be 0.

name: pidfd_open
notes: Real `Inode`-backed pidfd. Read blocks on target's `exit_waiters` queue (drained by every exit/kill path), returns 0 once the target is Zombie / KilledBy*. Poll returns POLLIN once exited. ESRCH for unknown pid. CLOEXEC by default. pid arg is ns-local; translates to host on entry. Pidfd identity is the host pid (so the pidfd survives ns crossing).

name: clone3
notes: Modern clone ABI used for `pthread_create` and `fork`. Reads `struct clone_args *` (any `size >= 64`; v0/v1 layouts both accepted), normalizes `stack BASE â†’ TOP` (clone3 differs from clone(2) here), then dispatches to a shared `do_clone` core. Strict-rejects `set_tid` / `CLONE_INTO_CGROUP` / `CLONE_CLEAR_SIGHAND` with `EINVAL` â€” silent acceptance would lie to userland.

name: close_range
notes: Closes every fd in [first..=last].

name: openat2
notes: Reads `struct open_how { flags, mode, resolve }`. `resolve` validation (unknown bits â†’ `-EINVAL`, per the ABI). `RESOLVE_NO_SYMLINKS` honored via `O_NOFOLLOW` for the final component (intermediate components fail at the existing path resolver if any segment is a symlink). RESOLVE_NO_XDEV / NO_MAGICLINKS / CACHED accepted as no-ops. RESOLVE_BENEATH / IN_ROOT need a sandbox-aware path resolver; workloads can use them today and fall back to the regular resolve.

name: epoll_pwait2
notes: Like `epoll_pwait` but with a `struct timespec` timeout. ns-resolution converted to ms via ceiling-divide (sub-ms timeouts round up so we don't return early), capped at i32::MAX. Same per-call sigmask handling as `epoll_pwait`.

name: futex_waitv
notes: The new primitive: park on up to 128 futexes at once, wake when ANY signals. Used by modern async runtimes. New `futex::wait_multi` enqueues the calling pid on each futex's `WaitQueue` (multi-queue park, same hook epoll_wait uses), parks via `park_self`, on wake dequeues from all + re-reads each *uaddr to identify which one changed. Returns the index (0..nr) on wake, -EAGAIN if any *uaddr already differs at registration, -ETIMEDOUT/-EINTR for the usual exits. Validates flags (FUTEX2_SIZE_U32 only â€” other sizes -EINVAL). 24-byte `struct futex_waitv` per entry. **Caveat:** per-entry mask is OR'd into a single bitset rather than gated per-entry â€” a relaxation of the spec. In the dominant case `mask == ~0` (any-waker-wakes-you, which is the only mask `struct futex_waitv` even has a field for) the behavior is identical; per-entry bitset-mask filtering would diverge.

name: set_mempolicy_home_node
notes: Single-NUMA correct: accepts `home_node == 0` (returns 0); any higher node is -EINVAL. Introduced alongside MPOL_PREFERRED_MANY.

name: fchmodat2
notes: Routes to `fchmodat`. The 4th `flags` argument (added 2023; `AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW` etc.) is accepted but not honored â€” we don't model symlink-vs-target-mode distinctly.

name: futex_wake
notes: Modern shape of `FUTEX_WAKE_BITSET`. Wraps `futex::wake_bitset` with the per-call `mask` arg directly exposed (legacy futex(2) buried it in `val3`). Validates FUTEX2_SIZE_U32.

name: futex_wait
notes: Modern shape of `FUTEX_WAIT_BITSET` with absolute timeout + clockid. Wraps `futex::wait_bitset`. Accepts CLOCK_REALTIME / CLOCK_MONOTONIC.

name: futex_requeue
notes: Modern shape of `FUTEX_CMP_REQUEUE`. `waiters[]` is a 2-entry `struct futex_waitv` array (src + dst). Wraps `futex::requeue` with the val from waiters[0] as the cmp value.

name: setxattrat
notes: `dirfd` honored â€” the `at` variants pass their dirfd through to the shared handler. AT_FDCWD resolves the path relative to the current working directory; a non-AT_FDCWD dirfd resolves relative to that directory fd. The `at_flags` argument (`AT_EMPTY_PATH`, `AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW`) is preserved-but-not-honored â€” the symlink-vs-target xattr distinction is not supported.

name: getxattrat
notes: Same shape â€” dirfd-aware.

name: listxattrat
notes: Same shape â€” dirfd-aware.

name: removexattrat
notes: Same shape â€” dirfd-aware.
